video_title: From Memecoins to Markets: Solana's Next Trade
gist: Lightspeed analysts discuss Solana's pivot from meme coins to tokenized equities, with Backpack's one-for-one fungible SpaceX shares as the headline catalyst; they assess market structure fragmentation risks, the Jito/JTX front-end opportunity, and the long-run convergence thesis where tokens simply become the superior issuance rail for equities.

themes.theme: Tokenized Equities on Solana: SpaceX as Catalyst

insights.claim: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX shares are one-for-one fungible with brokerage equities — KYC'd users can transfer them to and from traditional brokerages — making this structurally different from prior wrapped-asset products with uncertainty about the underlying.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: Fungibility removes the central counterparty uncertainty discount; if this model scales, it positions Backpack as the compliance layer between on-chain DeFi and regulated equity markets.
insights.quote: this is essentially the start of something very important. And yeah, then we can look into, I guess, like DEX dynamics and stuff like that as well, but the fact that this is now fungible

insights.claim: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX did ~$15M in spot volume on day 1, ~$7M on day 2, and ~$13M on day 3 — meaningful but dwarfed by Hyperliquid's perp market for SpaceX, which reportedly did over $1B in volume on IPO day.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: The perp market is ~70x larger than tokenized spot on day 1, signaling that derivatives infrastructure is far ahead of regulated spot tokenization; spot upside is large if regulatory clarity arrives.

insights.claim: Solana accounts for approximately 98% of tokenized-equity spot trading activity across all of crypto, making it the de-facto chain for real-world asset equity trading.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: Near-total market share in a nascent category is a strong moat signal; watch whether Ethereum-based competitors (e.g., Ondo on Base) can erode this.
insights.quote: as for actual spot activity for tokenized equities, they've been about like 98% of activity for all of crypto

insights.claim: Backpack's strategy mirrors the Circle/Tether playbook: push into a regulatory gray zone with a compliant product, accumulate regulatory hires (Can Sun, Mark Wetjen), and wait for the framework to catch up rather than waiting for permission to launch.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: If the regulatory analogy holds, first-mover compliant tokenized-equity platforms could dominate the way Tether dominates stablecoin market share; Backpack's regulatory hires are a leading indicator.
insights.quote: what Backpack is doing now with one-for-one fungible assets, um it's a similar dynamic. And they have a range of key regulation hires like Can Sun and Mark Wetjen

insights.claim: Superstate is partnering with Backpack to bring on-chain IPOs to market — a step toward disintermediating traditional investment banks as IPO underwriters, though the panelists note this is still unproven and hard to access.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: On-chain primary issuance would be a structural threat to bulge-bracket banks' IPO revenue; watch Robinhood (newly approved as IPO underwriter) and on-chain platforms converging on this opportunity.

insights.claim: Pre-IPO tokenized SpaceX (via pre-stocks SPV wrappers) is now trading at a discount to post-IPO spot price because of a ~6-month lockup on underlying share delivery and SPV fees, illustrating that pre-IPO spot tokens carry risks absent in perps or post-IPO spot.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: The discount creates an arb opportunity but also signals structural risk; investors in any pre-IPO tokenized asset should price in lockup duration, SPV fees, and share-delivery uncertainty before buying at par.

insights.claim: There are at least five distinct SpaceX token variants on Solana (Backpack Securities, X Stocks, Ondo tokenized SpaceX, pre-stocks, TSBPX) creating harmful liquidity fragmentation and user-experience friction that could deter institutional adoption.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: Fragmentation is the principal near-term risk to tokenized equity volumes; a standardization layer (e.g., Solana Foundation's tokens.xyz) or a dominant winner like Backpack's fungible model could be the key de-risking catalyst.

insights.claim: The Clarity Act is expected to enable institutional players to legally touch crypto-native tokenized equities; the panelists view its passage as the single largest unlock for on-chain equity volumes on Solana.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: Clarity Act passage is a binary catalyst for the entire Solana tokenized-equity stack (Backpack spot, JTX front-end, Radium/Meteora pools); monitor legislative progress as a lead indicator.

themes.theme: DEX Market Structure: AMMs vs. Prop Desks for Tokenized Assets

insights.claim: Traditional passive AMMs (Raydium, Meteora, Orca) currently dominate tokenized-equity spot liquidity on Solana, not prop AMMs, even though prop desks would theoretically have an edge quoting assets with live Nasdaq price feeds.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: If prop AMMs eventually enter once volume thresholds are met, passive AMMs will likely lose market share in the largest-cap tokenized equities — a negative for Raydium/Meteora revenue from those specific pairs.
insights.quote: these are great examples of something that a prop AMM would want to quote, right? It's like you have a very live, fresh, like up-to-date price from, you know, the Nasdaq

insights.claim: Prop AMMs currently quote mainly SOL-stablecoin and stable-to-stable pairs; their majority volume does NOT come from equity or long-tail assets, but recent high-bridge volume growth on Solana is prompting them to quote hi-bridge/USD pairs profitably.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: The hi-bridge / USD pair is a real-time test case: if prop desks become profitable quoting newly bridged assets, they will apply the same playbook to tokenized equities once those hit critical volume, compressing spreads and squeezing passive AMM fee revenue.

insights.claim: Long-tail tokenized equities (the 500th stock by market cap, or obscure names) will likely stay in passive AMM pools (Raydium, Meteora) permanently, because prop desks cannot justify infrastructure costs for low-volume assets — mirroring the meme-coin dynamic.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: Raydium and Meteora are the structural beneficiaries of the long tail of tokenized equities regardless of who wins the blue-chip pairs — a durable revenue stream if Backpack lists all US equities.
insights.quote: I guess like obviously they did very well during the meme coin activity. And if we do see like FTX Backpack essentially list all the stocks on the United States Stock Exchange... pools like Radium and Meteor could do quite well

insights.claim: Jump Trading, Securitize, and Jupiter are reportedly working together on tokenized equity market-making on Solana, potentially creating an institutional-grade liquidity arrangement similar to project-token deals at launch.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: If Jump provides institutional liquidity for tokenized equities via Jupiter, it would accelerate the shift of DeFi volume from meme coins to regulated assets and entrench Jupiter as the blue-chip routing layer.

insights.claim: Prop AMMs may require KYC infrastructure to quote tokenized equities (because brokerage KYC is a prerequisite for holding the underlying), making the cost of entry for quoting these assets structurally higher than for native crypto assets.
insights.speaker: Joe (advisor team)
insights.so_what: KYC friction is a moat for Backpack (already has the infrastructure) and a barrier for permissionless prop AMMs; compliance-native participants gain structural advantages in quoting these markets.
insights.quote: they have to KYC maybe on a on a brokerage or something like that and it's not worth the hassle until it's provably profitable

themes.theme: Jito / JTX: MEV Decline and the Pivot to Front-End Trading

insights.claim: Jito's core MEV/tips business is structurally declining as Solana's microstructure has shifted toward priority fees; Solana core developers and the Jito team itself envision eventually sunsetting Jito tips in favor of a single fee mechanism.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: Jito's block-building revenue is a melting ice cube; the JTO token's long-term value is conditional on JTX front-end success — investors should treat the token as a bet on a product-stage startup, not a stable MEV income stream.
insights.quote: Jito tips have been trending down and that's not necessarily Jito's fault, but is simply Solana's microstructure has shifted toward priority fees

insights.claim: JTX (Jito's front-end trading platform) targets a gap identified as 'no institutional-grade front-end on Solana today,' offering resting limits, bracket orders, OCO, TWAPs, and a maker-priority plugin — differentiated from existing retail-focused interfaces.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: If tokenized equity volumes scale, JTX is positioned as the order-routing and execution layer for institutional flows; this is a high-optionality bet contingent on Clarity Act and Backpack success.

insights.claim: Bull-case moon math for JTX: if Solana DEX volumes triple from ~$50B to ~$150B monthly and JTX captures 20% at ~2bps with a 20% router take, annual revenue would be ~$50-60M vs. a current annualized run rate of ~$5M — implying the current JTO multiple (~150x) could compress to under 10x.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: At 150x annualized revenue, JTO is pricing in a successful DEX volume tripling AND 20% market share for JTX — both aggressive assumptions; position sizing should reflect the binary nature of this outcome.
insights.quote: if we see Solana DEX volumes roughly triple from around 50 billion to 150 billion and say JTX gets 20% of that and say they are able to clip around two basis points... that roughly equates to uh around 50 60 million dollars per year versus the current annualized run rate of around 5 million

insights.claim: JTO token appreciated roughly 3x in the month prior to this episode, driven by market recognition of the JTX front-end pivot; panelists see a pullback risk from this compressed timeframe move.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: The rapid re-rating has pulled forward much of the easy gain; risk/reward for new entries is skewed unless Clarity Act passage or Backpack volume milestones provide a fresh catalyst.
insights.quote: wouldn't be surprised if we see a pullback from here given such a large move over the last couple weeks from around I think it's like roughly 3x in the last month or so

insights.claim: Jito's infrastructure edge — expertise in transaction inclusion and the maker-priority plugin — gives JTX a structural advantage over generic front-ends in providing best execution for institutional tokenized-equity traders.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: Jito's infrastructure stack is a defensible moat for JTX that generic UI competitors cannot easily replicate; watch for institutional flow migration to JTX as a confirmation signal.

insights.claim: The long-run end state is that tokens and equities converge into a single instrument: the token is simply a superior issuance rail (programmable, 24/7, global, transparent) for what is economically an equity, not a separate asset class.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: If this convergence plays out, the 'token vs. equity' debate is a category error; the relevant question becomes which issuance platforms and compliance rails win, not whether tokens survive.
insights.quote: I think it's not a matter of like we're going to see less tokens and more equity, but rather I think what we're all getting at is that they will both converge at some point and maybe the token is just a technology

insights.claim: In a fully tokenized equity world, the only remaining distinction for native crypto tokens (vs. tokenized equities) would be for true digital commodities like Bitcoin or L1 tokens that represent network participation rights rather than corporate ownership.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: This framework implies a bifurcation: digital commodities (BTC, SOL) retain a distinct identity while the vast majority of app-layer tokens will either become tokenized equities or become worthless — a bearish signal for governance tokens without clear equity rights.
insights.quote: the ideal end state in 10 20 years is equities and tokens are essentially the same thing as one completely synergistic system. Uh with maybe a distinction for like Bitcoin or an L1 in regards to being like a digital commodity

insights.claim: The Trump administration may ease US IPO regulations, potentially allowing companies to go public earlier and making tokens as pre-IPO access vehicles less necessary — a structural headwind for the 'token-as-early-access' investment thesis.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: If IPOs become easier and earlier, the liquidity premium that tokens command as pre-IPO vehicles compresses; investors using tokens as pre-IPO proxies should monitor regulatory easing in public equity markets.

insights.claim: Historical pattern: when a crypto company has both a token and equity, the token has been zeroed in virtually every observed case — via acquisition of the equity while the token becomes worthless — making the dual token-equity structure a strong bearish signal for token holders.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: Any project that issues both a token and retains equity (without explicit conversion rights) is a structural token short; only explicit conversion mechanisms (like Pump.fun's ACE round) have broken this pattern.
insights.quote: the largely the history that we've seen so far with these dual structures is that um the token effectively gets zeroed in most scenarios, right?

insights.claim: Pump.fun's ACE round pioneered a token-to-equity conversion mechanism for accredited US investors, becoming one of the first examples where token holders received actual equity rather than being zeroed out; Backpack appears to be following a similar path.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: The Pump.fun ACE round is the closest comparable for Backpack's BP token conversion promise; investors in BP should study Pump.fun's accredited-investor restrictions and one-time conversion structure as a risk template.

insights.claim: Second-order DeFi opportunity from tokenized equities: using on-chain equities as collateral in lending/borrowing protocols (money markets) — an already common TradFi use case (borrowing against equities) that has barely materialized on-chain despite Kamino and Jupiter listing tokenized assets.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: Lending protocols that accept tokenized equities as collateral are a second-derivative beneficiary of the RWA expansion; Kamino is the natural watch candidate on Solana for this use case.
insights.quote: I'm really curious to see if eventually like for instance people use equities as collateral on money markets and borrow against that. I think that's a very like low hanging fruit use case

themes.theme: Token Bootstrapping Value vs. Governance Failure Modes

insights.claim: Tokens remain valuable bootstrapping tools for products that have strong fundamentals but insufficient user acquisition: offering a token (or future revenue share) is a way to sacrifice future cash flows to acquire sticky early users, which can be positive EV if those users compound growth.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team), Sam (research team)
insights.so_what: Phoenix DEX (no token, promising perps product) is cited as a live case study in leaving growth on the table; a token launch there could be a meaningful volume catalyst and is a scenario to watch.
insights.quote: if you have a very good product, but you're struggling to get users, um offering a token or essentially future cash flows of the business... can be a very good way to bring in users

insights.claim: A Meta DAO project (Bee Chi neobank) reportedly saw ~10x user growth after its ICO on Meta DAO, illustrating the quantifiable bootstrapping power of token-driven community alignment.
insights.speaker: Carlos (research team)
insights.so_what: The 10x user figure, if accurate, implies token-launch user CAC can be dramatically lower than traditional growth spend; worth tracking Bee Chi's retention metrics to validate whether the users were sticky.

insights.claim: Crypto governance moved in the wrong direction historically by having token holders vote on granular operational decisions (inflation rates, validator onboarding); the correct structure mirrors corporate governance — executives make most decisions with token holders voting only on major items like executive selection.
insights.speaker: Sam (research team), Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: Protocols moving toward a CEO-led decision structure (rather than broad token governance) should be re-rated positively for execution ability; watch for governance minimization as a quality signal.
insights.quote: I feel like what um equities already have in place is like the correct structure. Like you need the CEO, a few executives making the key decisions

insights.claim: The prior era's push for rapid token distribution and community governance was largely a regulatory defense mechanism against SEC action — now that the regulatory environment has shifted, protocols are visibly and unapologetically re-centralizing decision-making while retaining revenue-share tokens.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: This structural re-centralization is a net positive for protocol execution quality but implies governance tokens (without revenue rights) are more exposed than before — another filter for token quality screening.
insights.quote: a lot of that uh movement was a bit um of an a negative for tokens in general because it just the the incentives were in place such that you were pushing the project to quickly like give up ownership

insights.claim: Decentralized infrastructure protocols (e.g., DePIN networks) are one of the few categories where a truly decentralized token model makes sense on first principles, because operator/user economics can be self-sustaining without centralized decision-making.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: DePIN tokens are the sub-category most defensible against the 'tokens should be equities' critique; they may command a structural premium over governance-only tokens as the convergence narrative plays out.

themes.theme: Prediction Markets: Geographic User Base and Sports Volume

insights.claim: Polymarket is seeing a significant uptick in World Cup trading volumes while Kalshi historically dominates US sports betting; the divergence tracks each platform's user base geography — Polymarket is more international, Kalshi more US-centric.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny), panelist
insights.so_what: Geographic user base is the primary driver of prediction market category dominance; Polymarket's international base is a structural advantage for global sporting events, while Kalshi owns the US sports calendar — both can grow without zero-summing each other.

insights.claim: Prediction market trading volumes have continued to grind upward across platforms and are increasingly dominated by sports gambling rather than the political or geopolitical categories that DeFi-native users initially expected to lead.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: Sports gambling as the dominant prediction market use case re-frames competitive analysis: Polymarket and Kalshi compete less with each other and more with regulated sportsbooks (FanDuel, DraftKings); monitor whether they gain regulated status.

themes.theme: Macro Context and Equity Issuance Surge

insights.claim: 2026 is shaping up as a record year for net equity issuance, driven by expected IPOs from SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic, and AI/semiconductor names; the surge is projected to blow past even 2021 IPO volumes and is being described as 'ending the era of stock scarcity.'
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: A record IPO wave is a direct volume catalyst for tokenized equity platforms on Solana; each high-profile IPO (especially AI names) is a potential demand spike for on-chain trading analogous to the SpaceX event.

insights.claim: The crypto market rally at the time of recording was partly attributed to Trump securing a deal with Iran, easing oil markets, which lifted equities and pulled crypto higher — illustrating the increasing macro correlation of crypto to risk assets.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: As crypto becomes more correlated to macro risk-on/risk-off via equity market linkages (especially as tokenized equities grow on-chain), crypto hedges using oil/geopolitical positioning become more relevant.

insights.claim: Robinhood has been approved as an IPO underwriter, signaling that fintech and crypto-adjacent platforms are directly encroaching on the investment banking IPO business — a structural threat to bulge-bracket underwriting fees.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: Robinhood's underwriter status plus on-chain IPO platforms (Backpack/Superstate) represent a two-front attack on traditional IPO intermediation; monitor for first on-chain primary issuance as a watershed event.

insights.claim: Solana Foundation is actively pushing asset trading on Solana via Frontier Traders, a campaign platform with trading leaderboards (e.g., SpaceX leaderboard with a $25K prize pool), marking a shift from 'build fast tech' to actively directing trading flow to the chain.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: Foundation-sponsored trading campaigns are a near-term volume catalyst; watch the Sunrise dashboard data to quantify how much of Backpack tokenized volume is incentive-driven vs. organic.

insights.claim: Backpack has a live token (BP) and has also signaled plans to IPO, creating a dual token-equity structure historically associated with tokens being zeroed out; the key mitigant is reported indications that BP stakers would receive equity allocation at IPO — but terms are vague and not legally enforceable.
insights.speaker: Host (Danny)
insights.so_what: BP token is a high-conviction short if Backpack IPOs without a formal, legally binding token-to-equity conversion; it is a high-conviction long only if a Pump.fun-style ACE conversion with clear terms is announced.
insights.quote: having two assets, equity plus token, um, typically is like not a good sign in the sense that if you're a token holder, you're probably a bit worried

insights.claim: The Backpack team would need equity holders to willingly dilute themselves for BP token holders with no legal enforcement mechanism — making the conversion entirely dependent on team integrity and goodwill, not contractual obligation.
insights.speaker: Toma / Joe (advisor team)
insights.so_what: BP token carries team-integrity risk as its primary risk factor above market or execution risk; due diligence should focus on whether conversion terms are codified in any legal agreement.
insights.quote: the equity has to be willing to take on this token that, you know, has a future state where it's worthless, and you have to be willing to be diluted for it. So, I think in the case of Backpack or like a Pump Cayd, you had equity that was willing to essentially dilute themselves in in good faith cuz there really is no legal enforcement to the token
